violations.text: Various foods in the large prep unit were observed held above 41 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods under 45 or recently stocked were moved to walk-in to rapidly cool. All other foods were disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/17/2026

The large prep unit was not in good repair. The ambient temperature was observed at 49 degrees. All food in unit was observed out of temperature. Unit taken out of service and must not be used until serviced, repaired, or replaced. Temperatures to be monitored once unit is in use. New Violation. Correct By: 03/17/2026
The refuse area was observed with garbage bags/debris underneath the dumpster. Area to be cleaned. New Violation. Correct By: 02/20/2025
The walk-in freezer was observed with Ice on the floor and in the vent. Walk-In freezer shall be in good repair to prevent ice build up. New Violation. Correct By: 02/20/2025

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 02/06/2024
